content.text: I'll start by reading the test file and current implementation to understand what's needed.

The failing test expects that `[1, 0]` (a dead cell) comes alive because it has exactly 3 live neighbors: `[0, 0]`, `[1, 1]`, and `[0, 1]`. The current implementation only filters existing live cells for survival -- it never creates new cells.

I need to add reproduction logic: find dead neighbor cells of live cells, check if they have exactly 3 live neighbors, and add them to the result.

content.text:
All 7 tests pass (2 remaining as todo).

Let me verify the final state of the implementation file.

Self-check: The only new logic added is reproduction -- finding dead neighbor cells of live cells and birthing those with exactly 3 live neighbors. This is exactly what the failing test demands and nothing more.

---

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added reproduction logic to `nextGeneration` in `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-11_02-19-03_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4.1-minimized_opus-4-6-portkey/src/game-of-life.ts`. The function now (1) collects all dead cells adjacent to any live cell as candidates, (2) checks each candidate's live neighbor count, and (3) births those with exactly 3 live neighbors, combining them with survivors.
